ocr.text: In the analysis of DNA extraction methods, Table 1 presents data for Uniplex (n=9), Quadruplex (n=1), and +Quintuplex (n=1),
showing that DNA extraction yielded approximately ∼16.5 for both Uniplex and Quadruplex, while +Quintuplex also yielded
∼16.5. The PCR results indicated approximately ∼71.0 for Uniplex and ∼16.0 for Quadruplex, with +Quintuplex showing the
same result of ∼16.0. Pyrosequencing results revealed approximately ∼141.5 for Uniplex, ∼31.5 for Quadruplex, and again
+Quintuplex showed ∼31.5.
Uniplex (n=9)  Quadruplex (n=1)
+Quintuplex (n=1)
DNA extraction  ∼16.5  ∼16.5
Pyrosequencing  ∼141.5  ∼31.5
Reference  Observed univariate statistics  Required statistics
X0*  Type Y0  Y0  n  mX  mY  sY  bYX  se(bYX)
The total values for these methods were approximately ∼229.0 for Uniplex, ∼64.0 for Quadruplex, and ∼64.0 for +Quintuplex.
Table 2 displays reference statistics, including observations for intervention and control groups. For reference [16], the
intervention had an X0* of 405, with a mean of 379 and SD of 189 from a sample size of 17, yielding mX of 6.00, mY of 5.83,
sY of 0.47, bYX of 0.12, and se(bYX) of 0.03. The control group recorded an X0* of 5 with a mean of 211 and SD of 77 from
17 samples, resulting in mX of 1.61, mY of 5.29, sY of 0.35, while bYX and se(bYX) were not applicable. Reference [17] for
intervention recorded an X0* of 505 with a median of 198 and IQR of 158-271 from 20 samples, yielding mX of 6.22, mY of
5.29, sY of 0.40, bYX of 0.13, and se(bYX) of 0.04, while the control had an X0* of 5, median of 110, IQR of 73-165, mX of
1.61, mY of 4.70, sY of 0.60, and no values for bYX and se(bYX).
